reasoning: Liverpool lost the first leg of their 2026 UEFA Champions League quarter-final 2-0 against defending champions Paris Saint-Germain away from home. The second leg will be played at Anfield, but overcoming a 2-goal deficit against a top-tier team is historically very difficult. Sportsbook odds have Liverpool at +450 to qualify, an implied probability of roughly 15-18% before adjusting for the bookmaker's vig (true probability ~14-16%). Furthermore, Liverpool is dealing with missing key players due to injury, most notably starting goalkeeper Alisson Becker and striker Alexander Isak. Market consensus on Polymarket rests solidly at 14%, which aligns closely with the betting odds and the situational difficulty. Therefore, I place the probability of Liverpool reaching the semifinal at 14%. (Note: Three identical probabilities are provided due to the N/A resolution date being parsed as three characters 'N', '/', 'A').
